title: santalum spicatum
intro: Santalum spicatum, also known as the Western sandalwood or Black sandalwood, is a species of sandalwood tree native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of santalol and other terpenoids. The health benefits of Santalum spicatum include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from pain and inflammation. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which are used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote relaxation and well-being.
